methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $375M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Quantinuum
private.rationale: Same sector (Quantum Computing), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B

data.narrative:
Photonic Inc, founded by Stephanie Simmons and Paul Terry, is a Series E quantum computing company that has raised $375M CAD to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 10 entities: 3 publicly traded peers (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 7 private-stage quantum computing companies including Quantinuum, IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and yields a $525M valuation by applying a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement to the $375M total raised proxy. This method reflects forward momentum expected at this stage. The Peer-Median approach is not applicable: while the quantum computing sector contains 7 private peers, the narrow same-stage and adjacent-stage filters contain insufficient datapoints (fewer than 3) to support a robust median calculation. Using an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given the specialized nature of quantum technology valuations.

The triangulated valuation range is $446M (low), $525M (median), and $604M (high), derived from single-method sensitivity rather than true cross-method triangulation. Key uncertainties include the sparsity of comparable Series E quantum companies and the elevated volatility in public quantum equities, which limits confidence in peer-derived multiples. The $525M median estimate is most defensible as the primary output, though the range reflects the limited granularity available in this early-stage, capital-intensive sector.
